title: Assign Urgency Level to Patient
description: The system shall allow a triage nurse to assign an urgency level from 1 (most urgent) to 5 (least urgent) to each patient based on their symptoms.
rationale: This requirement exists to ensure that patients are prioritized correctly in the queue, improving the efficiency and effectiveness of the healthcare service.
business_value: By assigning urgency levels, the system will help streamline patient flow, reduce wait times for critical cases, and enhance overall patient satisfaction.

source.description: As a triage nurse I need to assign an urgency level from 1 (most urgent) to 5 (least urgent) to each patient based on their symptoms So that the queue can be prioritized correctly

acceptance_criteria: Given a patient has been registered with symptoms
When the triage nurse assigns an urgency level from 1 to 5
Then the patient's priority in the queue is updated according to the assigned level
postconditions: The system updates the patient's urgency level in the database
The system recalculates and updates the patient's position in the queue based on the new urgency level
functional_constraints: Urgency levels must be between 1 (most urgent) and 5 (least urgent)
The system must store and retrieve urgency levels for each patient
The system must update the queue in real-time when a new urgency level is assigned

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should respond within 2 seconds to an urgency level assignment request
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle up to 100 patients simultaneously without degradation in performance
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, providing instructions for correcting input errors

edge_cases: What happens if a triage nurse assigns an urgency level outside the range of 1 to 5?
How does the system handle multiple simultaneous urgency level assignments?
What are the implications if the queue is temporarily unavailable during an urgency level assignment?
dependencies: The patient registration system must be functioning correctly and have up-to-date patient information.
The queue management system must be able to accept and process updated urgency levels.

interfaces.name: Patient Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the database to retrieve patient information, store urgency levels, and update queue positions.

interfaces.name: Queue Management API
interfaces.description: The requirement uses an API to communicate with the queue management system for updating patient priorities based on assigned urgency levels.

test_cases.description: Assigning a valid urgency level (3) to a registered patient.
test_cases.expected_result: The patient's priority is updated to 3 in the queue.

test_cases.description: Attempting to assign an invalid urgency level (<1 or >5).
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed, instructing the triage nurse to enter a valid urgency level.

test_cases.description: Simultaneous assignment of urgency levels by multiple triage nurses.
test_cases.expected_result: All patients' priorities are updated correctly in the queue, and there is no conflict or data inconsistency.
